steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a subject-verb agreement question. The subject is survey (singular), buried inside the long noun phrase “Her exhaustive chromosomal survey of plants in Silent Valley … (many of which are endangered).” The narrative is set in the 1970s, so past tense is required.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘are’: plural verb; cannot agree with the singular subject survey. Also wrong tense (present). ✗
  • Choice B — ‘were’: plural past-tense verb; still disagrees with the singular subject survey. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘have been’: plural present-perfect verb; disagrees with the singular subject survey. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘was’: singular past-tense verb; correctly agrees with the singular subject survey. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
